what_it_does: A research-findings markdown companion analyzing the existing decision->implementation wire (runtime/implement.py + suite.py) and answering whether a coherence finding-model could become an autonomous build loop's worklist; it concludes the wire's back half is built and proven but the front half (finding->intent origination, priority, burn-down governance) does not exist, and that convergence is defensible but not provable.
observations: States its area in the header and explicitly declines to re-describe the well-documented wire, spending words on the delta and the convergence claim.
Uses marking convention [OBS] observed file:line, [INF] inferred, [IDEA] net-new proposal.
Key structural fact: drive_dispatchable reads operator resolve verdicts (implement.py:512-513 FIFO by seq), not findings; detectors are never consumed by the loop.
Reports grep findings: no detector auto-originates a build-intent (only operator doors at bridge.py:1021 and surface_intent_at); no priority field exists anywhere.

Enumerates the 10-step gate stack dispatch_decision must clear (three-part bind, build-intent discriminator, exactly-once claim, posture gate, make-live-and-refresh, verify-by-use, FORM gate, scope-diff, git checkpoint, guarded close).
Identifies surface_intent_at (suite.py:6740) as the ~80%-built template for finding->intent origination.
Names three concrete thrash/non-termination vectors: infinite-retry on unfixable findings (no attempt counter), re-mint duplication (exactly-once keyed on seq not finding), detector-flap.
Argues detectors are gameable: a build can flip an orphan to 'wired' by adding a trivial test that mentions the route string, passing every existing gate (verification != resolution).
Frames the consent floor (resolve_surfaced operator-only, off the MCP face) as blocking autonomous burn-down unless a standing pre-authorization exists, which it does not.
standouts: The verification-is-not-resolution finding: the gate stack proves the build is sound but never re-runs the detector that raised the finding.
The detector-gaming attack (string-stuff a test -> orphan reads wired) that passes all current gates.
Reframing 'always completes' to 'burns down or halts loud' with a windowed burn-down governor, because provability requires empirical fix-monotonicity.
conventions: Holds the repo evidence discipline ([OBS]/[INF]/[IDEA]) and the no-silent-green / no-false-completion stance.
Reuses existing verbs in every proposal (surface_intent_at, _drive_dispatchable_bg, governance.POLICY) rather than new mechanisms.
Explicitly downgrades an absolute claim to an honest instrumented one.
concerns: Some claims are [INF] from reading rather than executed (e.g. the gameability path is reasoned, not demonstrated by a run).
Several dependencies (typed findings, accurate graph) are deferred to other areas, so the loop design is contingent on unbuilt substrate.
notes: The wire's back half (dispatch->verify->commit->close, exactly-once, revertible) is excellent; the front half (originator, priority, retry-cap, closure-check, burn-down governor, standing pre-auth) is the net-new work, and convergence can only be 'burns down or halts loud'.
questions: What is the ANCHOR.md whose sections (5, 7.1, 7.4) this file repeatedly engages and partly contradicts?
What are Areas B/C that own the typed-finding substrate and accurate connection graph this loop depends on?
Has the standing pre-authorization grant (the one consequential design call) been decided by the operator?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the header's question (how does a finding-model become the loop's worklist, and does always-completes hold) is answered directly, with the front-half-missing and convergence-not-provable conclusions.
apparent_themes: Verification of build soundness != resolution of the finding
Consent floor as structural, autonomy requires explicit scoped grant
Reuse the proven back half; build the small front half
intention_signals: Trying to honestly assess the autonomous loop: credit the proven wire, expose the missing originator/governor/closure-check, surface the detector-gaming and infinite-retry risks, and downgrade the convergence promise to a defensible instrumented claim.
novelty: The detector-gaming-passes-all-gates demonstration and the per-rule-readiness-style burn-down governor that converts an unprovable claim into a fail-loud non-convergence detector.
contribution: Contributes the back-half/front-half decomposition, the surface_intent_at originator template, the three thrash vectors with fixes (retry-cap, idempotent-per-finding, debounce), the closure-check-as-detector-rerun, and the standing-pre-authorization consent analysis.
summary_for_embedding: A coherence research companion analyzing runtime/implement.py's wire, finding its back half proven but the finding-driven front half (originator, priority, burn-down governor, retry-cap, closure-check) absent and convergence unprovable.
intention_for_embedding: To determine whether the coherence model can drive an autonomous build loop and whether the loop provably completes, honestly bounding the answer.
